segments.text: What advantage then has the Jew, or what is the profit of circumcision?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Much in every way! Chiefly because to them were committed the oracles of God.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For what if some did not believe? Will their unbelief make the faithfulness of God without effect?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Certainly not! Indeed, let God be true but every man a liar.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: That You may be justified in Your words, And may overcome when You are judged.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But if our unrighteousness demonstrates the righteousness of God, what shall we say? Is God unjust who inflicts wrath?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: (I speak as a man.)
Certainly not! For then how will God judge the world?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For if the truth of God has increased through my lie to His glory, why am I also still judged as a sinner?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And why not say, 'Let us do evil that good may come'?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: as we are slanderously reported and as some affirm that we say. Their condemnation is just.
What then? Are we better than they? Not at all. For we have previously charged both Jews and Greeks that they are all under sin.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: There is none righteous, no, not one;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: There is none who understands; There is none who seeks after God.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: They have all turned aside; They have together become unprofitable; There is none who does good, no, not one.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Their throat is an open tomb; With their tongues they have practiced deceit; The poison of asps is under their lips;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Their feet are swift to shed blood;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Destruction and misery are in their ways;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And the way of peace they have not known.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: There is no fear of God before their eyes.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Now we know that whatever the law says, it says to those who are under the law, that every mouth may be stopped, and all the world may become guilty before God.
Therefore by the deeds of the law no flesh will be justified in His sight, for by the law is the knowledge of sin.
But now the righteousness of God apart from the law is revealed, being witnessed by the Law and the Prophets,
even the righteousness of God, through faith in Jesus Christ, to all and on all who believe. For there is no difference;
for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God,
being justified freely by His grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus,
whom God set forth as a propitiation by His blood, through faith, to demonstrate His righteousness, because in His forbearance God had passed over the sins that were previously committed,
to demonstrate at the present time His righteousness, that He might be just and the justifier of the one who has faith in Jesus.
Where is boasting then? It is excluded. By what law? Of works? No, but by the law of faith.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Therefore we conclude that a man is justified by faith apart from the deeds of the law.
Or is He the God of the Jews only? Is He not also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, of the Gentiles also,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: since there is one God who will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through faith.
Do we then make void the law through faith? Certainly not! On the contrary, we establish the law.
segments.speaker: Paul
